title: Assistant Operations Manager - Remote| Singapore Company
description:
ASSISTANT OPERATIONS MANAGER — REMOTE | SINGAPORE COMPANY

Slim Sassy Strong Pte Ltd | Remote Full-Time | php20K–45K+ | Singapore travel opportunities



You’re good at your job. You’re just tired of the commute.

You’ve spent years working onsite — handling clients, managing workflows, keeping things running. You’re competent, you’re professional, and you show up.

But the traffic is costing you 2–3 hours every day. Hours you’d rather spend building something.

We’re offering you a way out of the commute — without stepping back in your career.



Slim Sassy Strong is a women’s wellness education company headquartered in Singapore. We are hiring one Assistant Operations Manager to join our remote operations team, with a structured path to Operations Manager and Senior Operations Manager.



This is a serious career role, not a work-from-home side gig.



|Phase   |Role                     |Compensation    |

|--------|-------------------------|----------------|

|Month 1 |Training                 |php20,000 fixed   |

|Phase 2 |AOM (KPI-based)          |php28,000 starting|

|Phase 3 |Operations Manager       |php35,000–php45,000 |

|Advanced|Senior Operations Manager|php45,000+        |



Plus: selected tea
mbers travel to Singapore for company events and operations.

Advancement is based on performance — not time served.



You will work directly with Singapore-based leadership as an operational extension of the business:

	-	Manage executive calendars and protect leadership time

	-	Own structured
 communication and client follow-ups

	-	Track KPIs, deadlines, and team task execution

	-	Conduct client-facing calls — professionally and confidently

	-	Support the consult pipeline and marketing-to-ops handover

	-	Maintain SOP compliance and operational documentation



As you grow into OM, you lead the team, own systems, and make decisions independently.

Client communication is a core part of this role — not an occasional task.



Why this works for you:

	-	No commute. Work from home, full-time, with a Singapore company on your resume.

	-	Real career track — AOM ? OM ? SOM with compensation that grows with your scope

	-	Singapore travel — top performers join us onsite for events and operational stints

	-	Above-market ceiling — ?45K+ for senior performers

	-	Long-term stability — we grow with people who perform; we don’t churn talent



You’ll do well here if:

You’ve worked onsite in operations, client coordination, admin, hospitality, or a similar field. You’re used to being relied on. You’re not looking for an easy job — you’re looking for a better setup to do your best work in.

Specifically, you:

	-	Are confident handling clients directly — calls, not just chat

	-	Stay composed and professional under pressure

	-	Meet deadlines without being chased

	-	Communicate clearly and directly, especially when escalating

	-	Are fast with digital tools and willing to learn new systems

	-	Want to build a career, not just collect a paycheck

	-	You don’t mind putting in extra hours when it counts. You just want those hours to actually mean something.



Technical requirements

	-	Minimum 50 WPM typing speed

	-	Strong written and spoken English — business standard

	-	Stable internet with a documented backup connection

	-	Own laptop suitable for business use

	-	Familiarity with Google Workspace or similar tools



How to apply:



 with the exact subject line:

Assistant Operations Manager Application

Include all of the following — incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

	1.	One paragraph: what you’ve handled onsite and why you’re ready to bring that to a remote leadership role

	2.	Your current city and power/internet stability details

	3.	Device specs + internet speed screenshot

	4.	Your backup plan during outages

	5.	A 1–2 minute informal audio introduction

	6.	DISC profile screenshot (OnlineJobs)

	7.	Typing test screenshot (min. 50 WPM)

title: Senior Google Ads and SEO Specialist
description:

You will be responsible not just for execution, but for strategy, performance optimisation, and continuous improvement, ensuring strong lead quality, efficient acquisition costs, and long term organic growth.

5+ years experience managing Google Ads campaigns with demonstrable results

Advanced knowledge of SEO strategy, including technical SEO

Strong experience with GA4, Google Ads, Google Search Console, and Tag Manager

Excellent written English and ability to communicate clearly with a remote team

I'm looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Sales Assistant to join me long-term and help me scale my sales output. This is not a temporary or short-term role I want to find someone I can train, grow with, and work with for years.



About the role:

Your main responsibilities will be (I will train you): 



- Drawing clean digital estimates based on my hand-drawn sketches and property photos 

- Pricing estimates using my templates and pricing logic

- Sending finalized estimates to clients on my behalf

- Updating and managing our CRM (GoHighLevel) to keep all leads, quotes, and follow-ups organized

- Sending contracts through our CRM

Communicating with me daily by
 about active jobs, requests, and priorities



- Over time, the role will likely expand into:

- Drafting personalized follow-up
s for specific clients

- Handling invoicing and deposit tracking

- Supporting marketing tasks (designing flyers, organizing assets, etc.)



What I'm looking for:

Strong attention to detail (this is the #1 trait sloppy work creates problems for our customers)

Must know how to use GoHighLevel

Spatial reasoning ability to look at a property sketch or photo and understand the layout

Comfort with digital tools (Google Workspace, PDF editors, CRM and AI like Claude or Chatgpt, basic drawing tools is a huge plus)

Professional written English and able to speak for our meetings (you'll be sending
s to clients on my behalf)

A long-term mindset I'm not interested in short-term arrangements

Willingness to learn through Loom training videos and weekly Zoom check-ins



Bonus if you have experience:

- Prior experience supporting a North American small business owner

- Construction industry 

- Sales experience

- Familiarity with GoHighLevel or similar CRMs (HubSpot, Pipedrive, etc.)

- Graphic design or basic Canva/Figma skills



What I offer:

- Above-market rate for part-time work I believe in paying premium for quality part-time engagements rather than competing on volume.

- Long-term stable work with a reliable, on-time-paying employer

- Direct relationship with me no agency middleman

- Clear training through Loom videos and direct guidance

- Weekly Zoom check-ins so you always know where you stand

- Opportunity to grow into more responsibility over time as we expand



To apply:



Please reply to this post with:



- A short introduction about yourself and your relevant experience

- Your full
-time availability and preferred working hours

- Examples of any relevant work experience you have



Please start your reply with the word EAVESTROUGH so I know you read this post carefully. Applications without this will not be reviewed.



I'm looking to hire within the next 1-2 weeks. Looking forward to hearing from you!



— Justin

title: Senior Civil Engineer
description:
We are an Australian-based engineering company seeking an experienced Structural/Civil Engineer to join our team in a long-term role, with the opportunity to grow into a leadership position. This role is well suited to someone currently working with another company — including those already supporting Australian firms — who is looking to transition into a more stable position with better long-term opportunities and growth. Experience working on Australian projects is essential, particularly within residential design.



Our work primarily involves residential projects, including project homes and custom homes, with a focus on efficient delivery and quick turnaround times. In this role, you will be responsible for managing a small team (approximately 3–4 staff), ensuring projects are delivered on time and to a high standard. You will oversee workflow, review outputs, and maintain quality control, while also contributing directly to structural design and project delivery yourself.



We are looking for someone who can take ownership, communicate clearly, and help drive team performance. A strong background in structural engineering is required, with additional experience in civil and stormwater being beneficial. This is a long-term opportunity within a growing business, and we are open to candidates seeking to transition from their current role into something more stable and rewarding. Salary is negotiable based on experience and capability, and we are flexible for the right person.



Holidays and benifits included

title: Customer Service VA - Mobile Rim Repair Business (Full-Time, Long-Term)
description:
About the business:

I run "The Rim Guy" — a mobile alloy-wheel repair business in Toronto, Canada. We drive to customers' homes and offices and fix curb rash, dents, scratches, and finish damage on wheels. The business runs on a custom CRM we developed: it handles quote requests from the website, customer SMS, and calls across 4 phone numbers (one per region), appointment booking, technician dispatch, and invoicing through Wave Accounting. You'll be using this CRM every day.



The role:

You'll be my front-line voice and the face of the brand. Because the business is named "The Rim Guy," customers expect to hear *the rim guy* when they call — so for brand consistency, I'm hiring a male VA with a confident, friendly phone presence.



What you'll do:

- Answer inbound calls and SMS from customers across our 4 city numbers

- Respond to website quote requests within minutes (the CRM auto-flags them)

- Qualify leads — wheel size, finish type, type of damage, customer location, urgency

- Book appointments in the CRM and dispatch the right technician

- Create and send invoices through Wave after each completed job

- Answer common questions ("how long does it take?", "can you fix a bent rim?", "how much for a diamond cut?")

- Log notes accurately so the technician shows up prepared

- Escalate anything weird or complex to me



Schedule:

- Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 6 PM Eastern Time (Toronto)

- This is roughly 9 PM to 6 AM Manila time — overnight shift

- ~40 hours/week. Possible paid Saturday shifts down the road.

- Only apply if you can do this overnight schedule long-term.



Required:

- Excellent SPOKEN English — clear, friendly, confident on the phone

- Strong WRITTEN English for texts and
s

- Reliable internet (50+ Mbps), quality headset, quiet work environment

- Comfortable with overnight Manila / Toronto business-hours schedule

- 1+ year of customer service experience, preferably with North American customers

- Tech-savvy — comfortable learning a custom CRM (web app + mobile app)

- Trustworthy with customer data and basic billing info

- Looking for a LONG-TERM role — I want someone to grow with the business



BIG BONUS if you have:

- Knowledge of cars and especially alloy wheels (curb rash, diamond-cut, powder coat, TPMS, offset, lug nuts, etc.)

- Hands-on experience at a body shop, dealership, detailer, tire shop, or auto shop

- Personal interest in cars

- Experience using any CRM, dispatch software, or appointment-booking tool

- Familiarity with Wave Accounting, QuickBooks, or similar

- Familiarity with Twilio, RingCentral, or any VoIP system



What I offer:

- Competitive salary (see wage field) negotiable based on experience and car knowledge

- 13th-month bonus paid in December

- Paid time off after 6 months

- Performance reviews every 6 months with raise potential

- Direct payment via Wise, PayPal, or Payoneer (your choice)

- Long-term partnership — I want to keep you for years, not months



HOW TO APPLY:

To be considered, your reply MUST include all 5 items below. Applications missing any of them will be skipped:



1. Your full name and a recent photo

2. A 2-minute audio recording introducing yourself and answering: "A customer calls and says, 'I scraped my front rim against a curb yesterday, and there's a chunk missing from the edge.' How do you respond?" (Loom, voice memo, Google Drive link, or any audio file works — I want to hear how you sound on the phone)

3. Your monthly salary expectation in USD

4. Self-rate yourself 1-10 on each of these: customer service, cars/automotive knowledge, English fluency, and learning new software

5. Start your reply with the word "RIMS," so I know you read the whole posting



Looking forward to building a long-term partnership with the right person.



— Nitzan, The Rim Guy

title: Stormwater / Civil Engineer

This position is ideal for someone looking for stability, consistent work, and the opportunity to be part of a supportive and growing team. You may currently be working with another company, including Australian clients, and are looking to transition into a role with better long-term benefits and a strong team environment.

You will be responsible for preparing stormwater designs, supporting project delivery, and working closely with our internal team on a range of projects including project homes and residential developments. ? YouTube / CTR experience 

? Curiosity gap obsessed





Apply ???? 





If you've ever caught yourself thinking "that thumbnail is the reason no one clicked" — this role was built for you.

title: SHORT FORM VIDEO EDITOR | Day Shift
description:
We’re hiring someone who knows how to turn long-form content into sharp, high-retention short videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.



This role is for someone who understands pacing, hooks, and visual rhythm in short-form content, and knows how to edit with intention rather than just cutting clips.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just cutting together videos for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have strong creative instincts and know how to spot 
nts that make people stop scrolling? Are you proactive, adaptable, and detail-oriented? Do you have a sharp sense of pacing, an instinct for hooks, and a clear understanding of how short-form videos need to flow to hold attention? Do you take pride in clean edits and delivering videos that feel intentional and finished? Do our core values sound like a fit for who you are?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Short-Form Editing & Story Selection

- Review long-form videos, transcripts, or raw footage to identify short-form clip opportunities

- Select 
nts that are clear, engaging, and platform-appropriate

- Edit vertical videos optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

- Create strong hooks, clean cuts, and natural pacing

- Deliver publish-ready videos that meet brand and platform standards



Captions, Visuals & Formatting

- Add on-screen captions (burned-in or SRT as required) with proper timing and readability

- Apply basic motion, zooms, punch-ins, and visual emphasis where appropriate

- Follow platform-specific formatting (aspect ratio, duration, safe margins)



Iteration & Workflow

- Implement feedback efficiently and meet agreed turnaround times

- Maintain clear file naming and folder organization

- Track edits, revisions, and delivery status as required



Requirements:

- A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION, REQUIRED (at least 150mbps)

- Experience with Final Cut Pro X (or other editing software, such as Adobe After Effects and Premiere Pro) is required

- Strong preference for MacOS users

- Experience editing short-form vertical content for social platforms, required

- Strong understanding of short-form pacing, hooks, and retention

- Experience working from transcripts or long-form content, required

- Clean file organization and understanding of post-production workflo

- Portfolio of completed short-form video projects, required

- General understanding of brand, content marketing, YouTube, etc, required

- GREAT interpersonal and communication skills, required

- Ability to self-start, work on a tight schedule, and manage multiple project deadlines, required

- Ability to follow directions and a LOVE of completing lots of projects according to consistent specifications, required

- Growth mindset, required



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.


 



STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $20-40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Short Form Video Editor with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your video editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: Pet Industry - Content & Production Specialist  (Full-time/Long-term/Remote)
description:
Hi there!



We’re hiring a Content & Production Specialist to support our Content, Editorial, & Social Systems team across posting, inbox management, and day-to-day operations.



In this role, you’ll be responsible for executing structured, repeatable tasks while supporting Content Managers with updates, live article edit coordination, and general content and production workflows. Your domain will be keeping content accurate, organized, and moving forward, while knowing when to take initiative and when to ask questions.



About us:



Pangolia is a fully remote, international company. We run high-traffic content and media websites that reach millions of readers each month, with a focus on the pet world.



About the Role:



You’ll be supporting multiple tea
mbers across content posting, live post edit workflows, inbox management, and operational tasks. Much of your work will follow clear processes — but not everything will.



This role requires someone who can balance accuracy, initiative, and communication. You should feel confident taking action within guidelines, but equally comfortable pausing to clarify when something doesn’t fit the usual process.



This is not a passive, task-only role. We’re looking for someone thoughtful, reliable, and communicative.



What You’ll Be Doing:



- Post and update content based on established guidelines

- Assist with live post edit workflows (applying flagged updates, formatting, and supporting content revisions)

- Help manage shared inboxes (sorting, flagging, and responding where appropriate)

- Follow structured processes for recurring tasks with high accuracy

- Support Content Managers with ad hoc updates and requests



What Success Looks Like:



- Content updates and posts are accurate and consistent

- Live edits are applied carefully and correctly

- Inbox communication is clear, timely, and organized

- Tasks are completed reliably with minimal to no errors

- Questions are raised at the right time — not too late, not unnecessarily early



Musts for the role:



- Available 40 hours a week 

- Strong written and spoken English

- Highly organized and detail-oriented  - In the first line of your application, please let us know what your favorite animal is (we are a pet company, after all!)

- Comfortable with repetitive tasks, but able to think critically when needed

- Experience with Google Sheets, Google Docs, WordPress

- Knows when to take initiative and when to ask for guidance

- Communicates clearly and proactively

- Experience in content, admin, or virtual assistant-style roles

- Reliable internet



Bonus (Nice to Have):



- Experience with social media posting or scheduling

- Basic video editing (short-form content, reels, simple cuts)

- Experience supporting content updates or edits

- Podcast production or promotion

- Outreach experience for interviews/guest articles

- An eye for content quality and presentation



What You Can Expect From Us:



- Clear processes and training to set you up for success

- Autonomy and trust once trained

- A collaborative relationship with the Content, Editorial & Social Systems team

- A role that will evolve as the team grows



Perks:



- Flexible work hours

- 10 PTO days annually

- 13-month bonus paid in December

- Public holidays off

- Internet reimbursement



To apply, please include:



- A brief introduction of yourself

- Relevant experience - have you worked in this area before?

- Why does this role interest you?

- Your CV 

- Anything else you think would help us understand how you'd thrive in this position



Can't wait to hear from you! 



Nicole Cosgrove

Director of Content, Editorial, & Social Systems

title: Paid Media Specialist (Google & Meta Ads)
description:
I. Position Overview

We are seeking a highly analytical and performance-oriented Performance Marketing Specialist to manage, optimize, and scale paid advertising campaigns across Google Ads and Meta 
 & Instagram) platforms.

This role plays a key part in driving qualified leads, customer acquisition, and revenue growth through strategic planning, hands-on execution, and ongoing performance optimization.



II. Key Responsibilities

Plan, execute, and manage paid media campaigns across Google Ads (Search, Display, YouTube, Performance Max) and Meta Ads platforms.

Conduct audience research, keyword analysis, and competitor benchmarking to improve campaign performance.

Monitor, analyze, and optimize campaigns to achieve KPIs such as CPA, ROAS, CTR, CPC, and conversion rate.

Collaborate with creative and content teams to develop and test high-performing ad creatives and messaging.

Allocate and manage advertising budgets effectively to maximize ROI and scale successful campaigns.

Implement and maintain accurate tracking systems using Google Analytics (GA4), Google Tag Manager, Meta Pixel, and other tracking tools.

Prepare weekly and monthly performance reports with actionable insights and optimization recommendations.

Stay updated on industry trends, platform updates, and emerging performance marketing best practices.

Work cross-functionally to align paid media strategies with overall marketing and business objectives.



III. Qualifications & Requirements

1–3+ years of hands-on experience in Paid Media or Digital Advertising.

Proven experience managing campaigns on Google Ads and Meta Ads platforms.

Strong analytical skills with a data-driven decision-making approach.

Experience with performance tracking, analytics, and reporting tools.

Ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously and meet deadlines.

Excellent communication and teamwork skills.



IV. Preferred Qualifications

Experience in lead generation and/or e-commerce performance campaigns.

Knowledge of A/B testing, remarketing strategies, and funnel optimization.

Experience managing medium to large advertising budgets.



V. What We Offer

Competitive salary with performance-based incentives.

Opportunity to work with diverse industries and fast-growing brands.

Dynamic and fast-paced performance marketing environment.

Continuous learning and professional development opportunities.

Clear career progression pathways.



VI. How to Apply

Please submit your CV to: 

Subject line: Performance Marketing Specialist – Full Name

title: BASE + COMMISSION: Perfectly Fast Moving – Long Distance Moving Carrier
description:
About Us:

Perfectly Fast Moving is a full-service long-distance moving company based in the U.S. Unlike brokers, we handle everything with our own trucks and crews. We are expanding our remote team and hiring motivated individuals to join us as Customer Sales Representatives.



Job Description:

You will begin as a call opener, engaging with potential customers and collecting their moving details. With training and strong performance, you will grow into a closer—handling full sales calls and securing bookings. This role offers a clear career path and high earning potential.



Responsibilities:



Answer inbound calls and make outbound follow-ups.



Gather customer information and moving details.



Provide excellent customer service with fluent, confident English communication.



Transition into a closer role to handle complete sales calls and secure bookings.



Work with a team to meet company goals and daily call targets.



Qualifications:



Fluent in spoken and written English (clear and natural accent required).



Strong communication and listening skills.



Sales background preferred but not required (training provided).



Must have a reliable computer, stable internet connection, and quiet workspace.



Motivated, coachable, and eager to grow into a closer role.



Work Schedule (Eastern Time):



Shift 1: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (1-hour lunch break)



Shift 2: 3:00 PM – 12:00 Midnight (1-hour lunch break)



Compensation & Benefits:



Base Pay: PHP 31,000 monthly



Commission: 5% per sale – top performers can earn up to PHP 240,000 monthly total (base + commissions)



PLEASE SUBMIT A VOICE RECORIDNG EXPLAINING YOUR WORK AND SALES EXPERIENCE, YOUR CURRENT WORK STATUS AND AVAILABILITY.



Daily training and support provided via Zoom



Referral program: We are hiring 50 people in total – if you have fluent English-speaking friends, you are encouraged to refer them

title: Graphic Designer, direct response
description:
We're a fast-scaling DTC pet supplement brand running heavy ad volume on Meta. We need a full-time graphic designer who lives and breathes the health and supplement space and knows what's working right now in paid social.

What you'll do daily:



Design static ad creatives for Meta — product shots, comparison images, offer graphics, testimonial cards, before/after layouts, sale banners

Create product images and lifestyle mockups for new product pages — hero images, ingredient graphics, dosing charts, trust badges, benefit callouts

Design new PDP image sets for product launches — everything a customer sees when they land on the product page

Build BOF offer creatives — bundle graphics, price slash images, free bottle callouts, urgency-driven sale assets

Create native-feel ad images that stop the scroll — product-on-counter shots, handwritten note setups, comparison layouts that look real not designed

Design packaging mockups and label concepts for new SKUs

Stay tapped into what's working in the DTC supplement and health space — follow competitors, save winning ads, bring new ideas to the table without being asked



You must have:



Proven experience designing for DTC health, supplement, or wellness brands

A portfolio showing static ad creatives that have actually run on Meta — not just brand work or logo design

Strong understanding of what converts in paid social — you know why certain layouts stop the scroll and others don't

Proficiency in Photoshop, Illustrator, Figma, and/or Canva Pro

Experience creating product page image sets for Shopify or similar platforms

Ability to create realistic product mockups and lifestyle composites

Eye for the native/UGC aesthetic — you can make something designed look undesigned when the brief calls for it

Fast turnaround — same day or next day on most assets

Bonus: experience with AI image tools like Midjourney or similar for generating base images and product composites



You should NOT apply if:



Your portfolio is logos, business cards, and brand guidelines

You've never designed an ad that ran on a paid platform

You don't follow DTC brands or know what's working in paid social right now

You need 3-5 days per asset

You've never worked in the health, supplement, pet, or wellness space



To apply send:



3-5 static ad creatives you've designed that actually ran on Meta or similar

1 example of a product page image set you've created

Which DTC supplement or health brands you follow and why

Your monthly rate



Don't send a generic cover letter or a portfolio link with no context. Answer the four points above and we'll talk.

title: B2B Lead Research + Outreach VA for Privacy Compliance SaaS
description:
am validating a new B2B SaaS product called BrokerDeleteOps.



BrokerDeleteOps is a compliance operations tool for registered data brokers preparing for California Delete Act / DROP processing. The product helps privacy, legal, compliance, and data operations teams manage recurring DROP workflows, including deletion-list intake, identifier matching, suppression-list tracking, service-provider follow-up, status reporting, and audit-ready evidence.



I need a detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to help with lead list creation, lead research, outreach support, and lead tracking.



This is not a generic scraping job. I need someone who can carefully research companies, identify whether they are likely data brokers, find the correct decision makers, and track outreach clearly.



Main Responsibilities

1. Build a list of target companies



You will research and build a list of registered or likely data broker companies.



Target companies may include:



Registered data brokers

People-search companies

B2B lead-data companies

Data enrichment companies

Marketing database providers

Adtech / martech data companies

Location-data companies

Identity graph companies

Audience data providers

Risk / fraud / identity data companies



You will use sources such as:



California Data Broker Registry

Vermont Data Broker Registry

Texas Data Broker Registry

Oregon Data Broker Registry

Privacy Rights Clearinghouse data broker research

Google search

LinkedIn

Company websites

Privacy policy pages

Data broker registration pages

Industry directories

2. Find the right people at each company



For each qualified company, find 1–3 decision makers.



Target titles include:



Chief Privacy Officer

Privacy Counsel

General Counsel

Compliance Officer

Data Protection Officer

VP of Compliance

Head of Data Operations

Data Governance Lead

Security/Privacy Operations Manager

CTO

COO

CEO / Founder, especially at smaller companies



Collect:



Name

Job title

LinkedIn URL

Work
 if publicly available or found through approved tools

Company website

Company privacy page

Notes on why they are a good fit

3. Score and qualify leads



Each company should be scored as:



A-list: Registered in California or clearly a data broker with strong DROP relevance

B-list: Likely data broker or registered in another state

C-list: Possible fit but unclear

Ignore: Not a data broker, consumer service only, law firm only, unrelated business



Strong-fit signals include:



Listed in a state data broker registry

Sells, licenses, shares, or provides consumer data

Offers people search, lead data, enrichment data, location data, audience data, or identity data

Has a privacy/legal/compliance team

Mentions CCPA, CPRA, opt-out, deletion, data broker registration, or consumer privacy requests

Has multiple datasets or data products



Do not add random privacy consultants, law firms, or consumer data-removal services unless they are clearly potential partners or influencers.



4. Maintain a clean Google Sheet



You will update a lead tracker with these columns:



Company Name

Website

Registry Source

Registered in California?

Other State Registries

Broker Category

Likely Data Type

Privacy Contact

General Counsel

Chief Privacy Officer

Compliance Contact

CEO / Founder

LinkedIn URL

Company Size Estimate

Current Privacy Tool Mentioned?

DROP Readiness Signal

Lead Score

Outreach Status

Checklist Sent?

Checklist Downloaded?

Reply?

Demo Booked?

Pain Confirmed?

Notes



Accuracy matters more than speed.



5. Help with outreach



You may help prepare and send outreach
s or LinkedIn messages.



The outreach will be checklist-first, not a hard sales pitch.



We are offering a free:



45-Day DROP Readiness Checklist



The goal is to find out whether companies are preparing for California DROP through:



Internal scripts

Existing privacy platforms

Manual spreadsheets

Legal/compliance workflows

Not sure yet



You will track who was contacted, who replied, who requested the checklist, and who may be interested in a discovery call.



6. Appointment setting



If a prospect replies with interest, you may help book a short discovery call.



Before booking a call, confirm:



They are a registered or likely data broker

They are involved in privacy, legal, compliance, data operations, engineering, or executive leadership

They are preparing for California DROP or responsible for data broker compliance

They are open to reviewing a prototype or checklist

Required Skills



You must be good at:



B2B lead research

Google search

LinkedIn research

Data entry

Google Sheets

Finding decision makers

Reading company websites and privacy policies

Following detailed instructions

Written English

Careful research and qualification

Nice-to-Have Skills



These are a plus, but not required:



Experience with B2B SaaS lead generation

Experience with compliance, privacy, cybersecurity, or legal tech

Experience finding privacy/legal/compliance contacts

Experience using Apollo, Hunter, 
, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Instantly, or similar tools

Experience with appointment setting

Familiarity with CCPA, CPRA, data brokers, privacy compliance, or opt-out/deletion requests

Important Quality Rules



Do not add companies that are clearly unrelated.



Do not add:



Random marketing agencies

Law firms, unless marked as possible partners

Consumer data-removal services only

Generic software companies with no data broker signal

Privacy blogs/news sites

Companies with no clear data business



Only add companies that appear to be registered data brokers or likely data brokers.



30-Day Deliverables



By the end of the 30-day project, I expect:



100–250 qualified target companies to start

At least 200–500 decision-maker contacts

Each company scored as A-list, B-list, C-list, or Ignore

Notes explaining why each company is a fit

Outreach status tracked

Replies tracked

Checklist requests tracked

Discovery call opportunities identified

Weekly progress updates



If the first 100 leads are high quality, we may expand the list to 500+ companies.



Weekly Targets

Week 1

Build first 100 company list

Find 150–250 contacts

Score leads

Identify top 25 A-list companies

Week 2

Expand list to 200+ companies

Continue finding decision makers

Start outreach support

Track replies and checklist requests

Week 3

Continue outreach and follow-ups

Identify interested prospects

Help book discovery calls

Clean and dedupe lead list

Week 4

Prepare final lead report

Summarize reply patterns

Identify strongest segments

Recommend next 100–250 targets

Outreach Message Example



You may help send messages like this:



Subject: 45-Day DROP readiness checklist



Hi [First Name],



I saw that [Company] is listed as a registered data broker.



I put together a 45-Day DROP Readiness Checklist for teams preparing for California Delete Act / DROP processing before August 2026.



It covers the operational workflow data brokers may need to map out: deletion-list intake, identifier matching, status classification, suppression-list updates, service-provider routing, status exports, and audit evidence.



Would it be helpful if I sent it over?



Best,

[Your Name]



How to Apply



Please answer these questions when applying:



Have you done B2B lead research before? If yes, what industries?

Have you researched privacy, compliance, legal, cybersecurity, or SaaS companies before?

Are you comfortable using Google Sheets and LinkedIn?

What tools have you used to find
s or decision makers?

How many qualified companies do you think you can research per hour?

Please explain, in your own words, what a data broker is.

Are you comfortable carefully reading company privacy pages and registration listings?

Put the word BROKERDELETEOPS at the top of your application so I know you read the full post.

Paid Test Task



Before hiring, I will ask shortlisted candidates to complete a small paid test.



Please find 10 registered or likely data broker companies and add them to a Google Sheet with:



Company name

Website

Registry source or reason they appear to be a data broker

Broker category

Privacy/contact
 if available

Decision maker name

Decision maker title

LinkedIn URL

Lead score: A, B, C, or Ignore

Notes explaining why the company is a fit



This test will help me evaluate research quality before starting the full project.

title: CUSTOMER SERVICES REPRESENTATIVE/RECEPTIONIST- LONG-TERM STABLE POSITION
description:
IMPORTANT: TO BE CONSIDERED FOR THIS ROLE, YOU MUST COMPLETE THE ASSESSMENT AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS POST. APPLICATIONS THAT DO NOT FOLLOW INSTRUCTIONS WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED.



About the Role



Are you an experienced Customer Services Representative / Receptionist with excellent communication skills and confidence on the phone? Do you speak and write in fluent English? Are you looking for a long-term, stable position with opportunities for growth?



If yes, this role could be the perfect fit for you.



As our Customer Services Representative / Receptionist, you will be the first point of contact for our customers. You’ll play a key role in providing phone support, managing messages and
s, scheduling appointments, and carrying out essential administrative tasks.



We are a UK-based business seeking committed, detail-oriented individuals who are eager to stay and grow with us for years to come.



Key Responsibilities



• Be the first point of contact on the phone

• Handle inbound and outbound calls with professionalism

• Manage
s and messages via Outlook

• Carry out administrative and data-entry tasks

• Schedule meetings and maintain Outlook Calendar

• Support basic bookkeeping tasks and record-keeping

• Prepare and organise PDFs, documents, and schedules

• Proofread communications and ensure accuracy



Working Hours



• Full-time only – 40 hours per week

• UK office hours: 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM UK time (3:00 PM – 12:00 AM PST) (Saturday and Sunday off)

• Commitment: This role is for candidates who will work with us exclusively (no other jobs or freelance work).



Salary & Benefits



• Starting salary: 40,000 PHP/month

• After probation (based on performance): 45,000 PHP/month or higher for experienced candidates

• Paid day-offs during UK holidays (after probation)

• Monthly and quarterly bonus programme based on length of service



Tools & Communication



• Daily communication via Microsoft Teams

• Time tracking with TimeDoctor

• You must have a fast, reliable internet connection, Windows laptop/PC (minimum 8GB RAM), and a quiet, professional workspace



Requirements



• Own computer or Laptop (Intel core i3, minimum 8GB RAM, Windows OS)

• High-speed and stable internet access of not lower than 100 Mbps

• Quiet and private working space without any background noise

• Noise-cancelling headset

• Knowledge of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook etc.)

• Exceptional verbal and written communication skills

• Excellent telephone skills that connect with people and display personal credibility

• Detail-oriented and highly organized

• Ability to work independently and solve problems proactively



Common Questions



Is this a full-time role? – Yes, 40 hours per week.

Do I need to work fixed hours? – Yes, UK office hours (8 AM–5 PM)

Can I work for another employer at the same time? – No. We are a committed team so we ask that you commit to only working with us.

Will my calls be monitored? – Yes, for training and quality purposes.



How to Apply



Step 1: Apply to this post with the subject line: “I love helping on the phone!”

Step 2: Attach your most recent CV highlighting relevant experience (we value candidates with long-term roles and proven stability).

Step 3: Ask us at least one question in your application. The more questions, the better—we value curiosity and engagement.

Step 4: *MOST IMPORTANT* Record a 60-second video answering the questions below. Upload your video to your preferred platform and include the link in your application.



Video Questions:



1. Do you speak perfectly fluent English?

2. How fast is your internet connection? Send a screenshot from ---------- .

3. Will you be able to work 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM UK time?

4. When could you start?

5. Let us know what you want to achieve from this job long term.

6. Do you love helping customers?

7. What is the most important part of being a customer service representative?

8. Are you comfortable reporting your progress to the team on a daily basis?

9. Are you happy for your calls to be monitored for training?



This is a serious, long-term opportunity for the right candidate who values stability, growth, and being part of a professional, supportive team.



If you’re ready to commit and grow with us, we’d love to hear from you.

title: AI UGC Editor (Higgsfield Specialist)
description:
We’re looking for a highly skilled AI UGC Editor who can create realistic, high-converting AI-generated UGC ads using Higgsfield.



This is not a traditional video editing role — you will not appear on camera or record yourself. Everything is created inside AI tools. Your job is to bring scripts to life using ultra-realistic AI avatars that feel indistinguishable from real creators.



What You’ll Be Doing:



Create AI-generated UGC-style ads using Higgsfield

Direct AI avatars to perform natural, believable actions (not just talking)

Build scenes that feel real (e.g., car interiors, natural lighting environments)

Ensure lip sync, pacing, and delivery feel human

Produce scroll-stopping creatives designed for Meta & TikTok ads

Deliver ready-to-use video outputs (with basic formatting via CapCut if needed)



What Great Work Looks Like:

• Avatar realism: The AI character must look like a credible, attractive real person — not an obvious AI. No uncanny valley.

• Action execution: The editor must be able to direct the avatar to perform specific physical actions on cue — putting on or taking off glasses, glancing out a window, reacting with surprise or delight. This requires Higgsfield skill, not just template use.

• Natural delivery: Lip sync must match the script. Pacing must feel human. No robotic cadence or dead eyes.

• Setting and lighting: The scene needs to feel like a real environment — typically a car interior with natural window light. The editor must be able to build or select scenes that match the brief.

• Ecom awareness: The editor should understand what a direct-response ad needs to do — stop the scroll in the first 3 seconds, hold attention, and feel like organic UGC, not a produced commercial.



Non-Negotiable Requirements:

• Proven Higgsfield experience — must be able to show prior work, not just claim it

• Ability to generate realistic AI avatars that pass as real people at first glance

• Ability to direct avatar actions beyond just talking — physical movements, reactions, props

• Ecom or DTC advertising experience — they must understand what a Meta/TikTok ad needs to do

• Basic CapCut familiarity — even if Faiz handles final edit, the editor should understand the output format

• Fast turnaround — able to deliver a complete Higgsfield video within 48 hours of receiving a brief

• Reliable communication — active on WhatsApp and 



Strong Bonus (Nice to Have)

• Portfolio of AI UGC ads that have run on Meta or TikTok

• Experience with product-demo style AI video (showing a product being used by an AI avatar)

• Has worked with ecom brands in health, beauty, lifestyle, or accessories categories

• Understands hook structure and first-3-second creative principles



Automatic Disqualifiers:

• Cannot show any real Higgsfield output — claims experience but has no work to show

• Output looks clearly AI-generated / uncanny — avatar is not convincing as a real person

• No understanding of what a direct-response ad is trying to accomplish

• Cannot direct avatar physical actions — only knows how to use basic talking-head templates

• Slow responder — takes more than 48 hours to reply during the hiring process itself

• Asks to see scripts or briefs before the test task is completed



Shortlisted candidates will be given a paid test task to evaluate fit for the role.



How to Apply

Please include:

Portfolio of Higgsfield work (required)

Examples of AI UGC ads (especially for Meta/TikTok if available)

A short note on your experience with e-commerce creatives

Your typical turnaround time



Application Instructions (Important)

Do not send applications through OnlineJobs.ph messaging system or
 — these will not be reviewed.

To apply, contact directly via WhatsApp: Joan 
Important:

This role is for someone who understands that performance matters more than aesthetics.

We’re not looking for “nice-looking AI videos” — we’re looking for ads that convert.

title: SMS Chat Support Specialist
description:
As an SMS Agent, you'll be sending messages in SMS conversations and your role is to turn these conversations into happy customers/opportunities.



We have 1:1 SMS conversations with customers who have expressed interest in a product and our job is to have fun conversations, learn more about them, educate them on our client's offering and try and get them to sign up.



You're not talking to angry support tickets all day, but instead talking to people who are genuinely interested in conversing.



What Sets Us Apart:

We offer a generous bonus incentive system where your earnings can substantially increase based on your ability to turn leads into loyal customers. On average, our agents bonuses are around a 30% bonus on top of their hourly pay.



What We Value:

At SmartRecover, we appreciate a quick learner with common sense and resourcefulness. While we're here to support you, the ability to absorb information rapidly and find answers independently is highly prized.



Your Qualifications:

Proficiency in conversational, casual English.

Problem-solving abilities to handle a variety of customer queries on the fly.

A genuine affinity for people and a talent for building rapport.

Are self motivated



Note: Sales experience is not mandatory, but it's definitely an advantage. If you've worked in sales before, you'll find your skills are transferable and valuable in this role.



What We're Looking For:

Your primary role is to craft friendly, casual, and supportive responses to users. We emphasize empathy and a personal touch in every conversation, prioritizing a human-like approach over a robotic or overly formal tone. Understanding the customer's preferences, needs, and emotions is key. Our ideal agent possesses a lighthearted, conversational, and friendly style.



The Application Process:

To be considered for this position, include a loom video or other video recording of yourself describing your experience and why you would be a great fit for this role. Please limit length to 2 minutes and upload here:

Pro tip: We're less interested in a rigid list of personal accomplishments and more interested in seeing your casual personality shine through:)

title: Content Strategist & Manager
description:
We are looking for a Senior Content Strategist and Social Media Manager who brings deep, earned expertise to this role. Someone who already knows how social media works at a strategic level — the psychology behind why content stops the scroll, the difference between awareness content and conversion content, why what works on one platform fails on another, and how to build a content strategy that actually grows an account and drives sales.



This is a collaborative role. You will work directly with Keenya. She shares what is happening in the business — the offers, the webinars, the content drops, the priorities — and you build the strategy on top of that. You bring the thinking. You bring the plan. You do not wait to be told what to post.



What We Learned From Our Last Hire

We have been through this search recently and we know what we are looking for. The person we want understands the psychology of content at a deep level. They know the difference between content that builds awareness with cold audiences and content that converts warm audiences into buyers. They can look at a business and immediately understand how to translate what is happening in that business into a content strategy that grows and sells. If that description fits you, keep reading.



What You Will Do

Content Strategy

Receive a weekly briefing from Keenya and build a full multi-platform content strategy around it — without being told what to post, how many times to post, or what angle to take



Come to every week with a proactive plan that accounts for growth content, authority content, and conversion content across every platform



Understand the audience at each stage of the funnel — cold audiences who have never heard of Keenya, warm audiences who follow but have not bought, and existing buyers who need to ascend — and write content that speaks correctly to each stage



Think about content the way a marketer does — every post has a purpose, a target, and a measurable goal

Identify what is working, what is not, and bring data-informed observations to Keenya proactively



Content Creation

Write captions, scripts, and hooks for all platforms — formatted, platform-specific, and ready to use with no reformatting needed



Write short-form video scripts and hooks that Keenya can film — with awareness versus conversion content clearly distinguished in your approach



Write promotional content for webinars, offers, challenges, and launches that is built to convert, not just inform



Write in Keenya's voice — direct, warm, faith-forward, and conversational. Not in your voice. Not in a marketing template. Hers.



Adapt content intelligently across platforms — what you write for
 is not what you write for YouTube, and you know why



Platform Management



Manage the content calendar and keep it one full week ahead at all times



Schedule and publish content on all managed platforms on time, every time



Promote the newsletter, both podcasts, and YouTube content every single week without being reminded — this is a standing, non-negotiable responsibility



Engage with comments and community interactions on managed platforms



Monitor platform performance and bring strategic observations to Keenya weekly



Weekly Planning & Communication

Submit a complete weekly content plan every Monday — a real strategic plan, not a list of post topics



Bring at least one proactive strategic insight or content idea each week that Keenya did not ask for



Communicate proactively and clearly — no going quiet, no missing deadlines without early notice



Study the business before building the strategy — you cannot build a strategy for a business you have not taken the time to understand



Who You Are

You have years of real social media strategy experience — not just posting experience, but strategic experience where you have been responsible for account growth and conversion results



You understand the psychology of content deeply. You know why certain content stops the scroll, why certain hooks work on cold audiences and others do not, and why awareness content and conversion content require completely different approaches



You can look at a business and immediately start thinking about how to translate what that business does into a content strategy that grows and sells — without needing everything handed to you



You are the person in the room who knows things others do not. You bring ideas, frameworks, and strategic perspective that add real value



You write well. Captions, scripts, hooks, promotional copy — you do all of it at a high level and you do it in someone else's voice without it sounding like you



You are proactive by default. You do not wait to be told. You bring the plan, you flag the opportunities, you ask the questions that need asking before problems happen



You thrive in a fast-paced entrepreneurial environment where priorities shift and speed matters



What We Mean By Awareness vs. Conversion Content

This distinction matters to us and we want anyone applying for this role to understand it clearly before they apply.



Platforms

You will be building strategy for and managing content across multiple platforms. The exact scope will be confirmed during the interview based on your experience and strengths.



Platform

Notes


64,000+ followers. Relationship and conversion platform. Your primary execution platform.

YouTube

9,600+ subscribers. Authority and education platform. Strategy and promotion.

LinkedIn

5,400+ followers. Thought leadership platform. Consistent posting and repurposing.

Threads

Active account. Conversational, insight-driven content.

Instagram 33,800 & TikTok 514,000

You will write scripts and hooks for these platforms. Keenya manages all filming and posting on both herself.





Tools We Use

Tool

Purpose

ManyChat

DM automation and comment keyword triggers on Instagram and

Canva

Graphics, carousels, and visual content creation

Metricool

Scheduling and analytics

Notion

Content calendar and project management

Meta Business Suite

 and Instagram management

YouTube Studio

YouTube content management

Google Drive

File storage and team collaboration



How to Apply

To be considered, please submit all of the following. Applications missing any of these will not be reviewed.



A brief introduction — who you are, your background in social media strategy, and why this role fits where you are in your career

Two or three examples of your strategic work — content calendars, strategy documents, campaign plans, or written content you have produced for a client or employer

title: Remote VA / Project Coordinator
description:
Mission Control (Remote VA / Project Coordinator – Travel & YouTubeThis is not a typical role



This is a front-row seat to a real-world expedition



The Mission:

I am a veteran IT professional with 36 years of experience, currently preparing for a solo motorcycle and hiking expedition: The Stoic Solo Hiker. This 8-month, 15-island journey across the Philippines documents endurance, culture, and Stoic philosophy. I’m building a system that allows me to stay focused on the road and the story.

The journey begins in Teachers Village, Quezon City, and transitions into continuous travel across the country. This engagement supports the systems, logistics, and production behind that mission.

________________________________________

The Professional Partnership (Collaboration & Growth):

This is not a traditional administrative role. It is a high-level professional collaboration built on mutual respect, trust, and independent thinking.

I am seeking an independent professional who operates with strong judgment, initiative, and accountability. You will not be following a rigid checklist. Instead, you will provide independent support, insights, and solutions that help navigate an evolving and dynamic environment.

This role is best suited for someone who is mapagkakatiwalaan—reliable, composed under pressure, and capable of solving complex problems with both precision and professionalism.

________________________________________

Core Responsibilities:

• Technical Logistics: Provide independent coordination and research for real-world logistics, including ferry routes, mountain access, permits, and route planning. Identify risks and recommend solutions to maintain continuity of travel.

• Production Management: Independently manage the YouTube production workflow, including uploads, metadata optimization, thumbnails, and publishing schedule, ensuring consistent and high-quality output.

• Calm Under Pressure: Maintain professional composure when plans change. Assess situations objectively and provide clear, actionable solutions to keep the project moving forward.

• Lifestyle & Social Scouting: Identify and recommend culturally relevant venues, live music locations, and events that enhance storytelling and on-camera experiences, with a focus on cinematic and authentic local appeal.

• Strategic Alignment Support: Provide independent input to help maintain alignment with project goals and priorities. Offer perspective that simplifies complexity and supports effective decision-making in dynamic situations.

• Conversational Language Coaching: Support conversational practice in Filipino (Tagalog) and basic Bisaya as part of regular communication during the preparation phase.

________________________________________

Qualifications:

• Multilingual: Excellent English and Tagalog required; Bisaya (Cebuano) is an advantage

• Technically Ambitious: Interest in learning tools related to video production and digital workflows

• Proactive Problem Solver: Identifies issues early and proposes solutions independently

• Strong Cultural Awareness: Communicates naturally and effectively across contexts

• Education: Degree (completed or in progress) or equivalent real-world experience

• Lifestyle Alignment: Active, curious, and interested in showcasing the Philippines authentically

________________________________________

The Pay & Engagement Structure (Clarification):

• Retainer Phase (Now – Sept): Estimated ~6 hours per month (training, research, language practice)

• Expedition Phase (Oct – June 2027): Estimated ~5–10+ hours per week depending on project needs

• Engagement Type: This is an independent contract engagement. The contractor retains full control over how services are performed. No employer-employee relationship is intended.

• Service Fee: $6–$7 USD/hour + monthly Connectivity & Communications Stipend

________________________________________

This role is NOT for you if:

1. You require constant direction 

2. You overlook details or ignore instructions 

3. You prefer passive work over problem-solving and ownership 

________________________________________

How to Apply (Screening Test):

Send your application to 

Include a link (Loom, YouTube Unlisted, or Google Drive—no attachments) to a 1–2 minute video covering:

1. The G2 Challenge: Explain the technical and physical difficulty of Mt. Guiting-Guiting 

2. The Logistics: Current Ro/Ro ferry options for a motorcycle from Batangas to Sibuyan Island 

3. The Social Scout: Recommend one high-quality live music venue for the Pinoy Indie (OPM) scene 

4. If applicable: How this engagement aligns with your long-term goals 

Subject Line: Mission Control Application – [Your Name] – [Site you found it on]

Incomplete submissions will not be reviewed.

title: LinkedIn Outreach Assistant — 24-Day Project, ~5 Min/Day
description:
WHAT'S THE WORK?



We're hiring 40+ part-time assistants for a 24-day LinkedIn outreach campaign starting late April 2026. The work is 100% copy-paste: open a candidate's LinkedIn profile, paste a pre-written InMail message, send, mark complete in a tracking sheet.



- ~5 minutes per day

- ~3.5 hours total over 24 days

- 100% scripted — no message writing, no replies, no decisions

- Pre-prepared queue delivered to you daily

- You will NOT respond to candidate replies — you forward them to our internal team

- Skip days are fine. Weekends, family stuff, sick days — your queue rolls forward, just keep up roughly with the daily pace overall





WHAT YOU EARN



$0.35 per message verified. About 75 messages over the campaign = $26.25 total.



Choose how you get paid:



- WEEKLY PAYMENTS via USDT (Solana network), bank transfer, or mobile top-up — paid every Friday

- OR SINGLE PAYMENT at the end of the campaign + $5 completion bonus = $31.25 total



Top performers also get first invitation to Phase 2 and Phase 3 of this campaign over the following 60 days (additional ~$30-50 each, not guaranteed).





WHAT YOU NEED



1. A LINKEDIN PROFILE IN ONE OF THESE FIELDS: recruiting, talent acquisition, HR, executive search, executive/personal assistant, or virtual assistant. Profiles in unrelated fields (graphic design, marketing, sales/SDR, lead generation, software development, healthcare, customer service, etc.) will not be considered — even if your past work history includes some recruiting experience. Your current LinkedIn headline matters most.



2. A LinkedIn account at least 1 year old, with a professional photo and (ideally) 50+ connections.



3. YOU HAVE NOT USED A LINKEDIN SALES NAVIGATOR FREE TRIAL IN THE PAST 12 MONTHS (we'll verify with a 2-minute screenshot check before hiring).



4. ~5 minutes most days for ~24 days.





ABOUT US



We're Obsidian Global, a business group running this campaign for three companies. Our coordinator Piumantha is your main point of contact.



We pay every freelancer who completes the work, on time, in full, via your chosen method. Weekly payments option exists specifically for freelancers who prefer regular payouts.





HOW TO APPLY



Reply with:



1. A SCREENSHOT OF YOUR LINKEDIN PROFILE (showing photo, headline, current role, connection count)



2. Confirmation: "I have NOT used a Sales Navigator trial in the past 12 months"



3. ONE SENTENCE ANSWERING THIS: What is our policy on responding to candidate replies?



Piumantha responds to qualified applicants within 24 hours during weekdays.

title: Supply Chain & Logistics Coordinator — E-commerce, Multi-Market
description:
We're a fast-growing e-commerce brand selling across the US, UK, EU, Australia, and Canada. We're hiring a sharp, detail-oriented coordinator to own our inventory and supply chain operations day-to-day — from monitoring stock levels to negotiating freight quotes and keeping Amazon FBA topped up across markets.

This is a full-time, long-term role at $1,000/month USD, paid via Wise.

What you'll own



Monitor our inventory spreadsheet daily, identify what needs reordering, and create purchase orders

Coordinate with suppliers in China and warehouses in our markets —
s, follow-ups, shipping schedules

Manage Amazon FBA replenishment: build shipment plans, track inbound, prevent stockouts before they happen

Source freight forwarders, request and compare quotes from multiple sources, and negotiate to save us money on every shipment

Run cost analysis and bring decisions to the founder with options and recommendations (which SKU to reorder this week, which forwarder to book, where margin is leaking)



You're a great fit if you:



Have 1+ years of experience in e-commerce operations, supply chain, inventory management, or a similar role (any industry counts — Amazon, Shopify, retail, freight, manufacturing, fuel/utilities)

Are comfortable reading spreadsheet data and making decisions from it

Can write professional
s to suppliers, warehouses, and freight forwarders

Are self-managed — we train with Loom videos and written SOPs, then trust you to run

Have used at least some of these tools: Google Sheets / Excel, Shopify, ShipStation or Shiphero, Amazon Seller Central, Wise

Speak and write English clearly enough to handle US and overseas communication



The setup



Full-time, $1,000/month USD, paid via Wise

Work your local hours — we'll need about 5 hours/week of overlap with US Pacific Time for daily check-ins and the occasional sync call

Long-term role; we're hiring someone to grow with the business

We invest in onboarding: SOPs, Loom walkthroughs, weekly 1:1s



To apply

Please complete our 15-minute skills pre-screen here: 

The form is auto-scored. We review every submission and respond within 5 business days. If your score is strong, we'll invite you to a 30-minute video interview.



Important: please do NOT use AI tools to complete the form. We're testing how YOU think about real problems, and AI-generated answers are easy to spot. Honest answers — even when you don't know — are always better than polished guesses. Candidates who clearly used AI will be declined regardless of score.

title: Dental AR Specialist — Patient Collection Calls ($4-5/hr) — AI-Assisted Prep, US Practice
description:
We're 5D Smiles — a dental implant practice in Downey, California — hiring a remote Dental AR Specialist to make patient collection calls (the empathetic kind, not the harassing kind).



This is the role for someone who can have a HARD conversation softly. You'll call patients about $500-$5,000+ balances, uncover why they haven't paid, and either set up a payment plan or get the money. The patients are real people in real situations — sometimes hardship, sometimes just busy, sometimes dispute. You need both empathetic firmness AND dental billing literacy.



WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO:

- Call patients about outstanding balances (30/60/90+ day aged AR)

- De-escalate disputes ("you guys overcharged me!")

- Negotiate payment plans (autopay, down payment, terms)

- Explain charges to patients in plain English (D2740 = "your crown")

- Document every call in Open Dental

- Coordinate with billing VA on insurance disputes

- Use AI to prep before each call — pull chart, EOB, prior notes (we'll teach you the workflow)



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

- 2+ years collections, AR, or patient-facing billing experience

- Empathetic firmness — you don't get pushed around but you don't push patients around either

- Dental billing literacy (CDT codes, EOB reading, insurance basics) — strongly preferred but trainable

- Comfortable making outbound calls all day (this is a phone-heavy role)

- Fluent English (Spanish bonus — many of our patients are bilingual)

- Reliable internet, quiet workspace, USB headset

- Available 40hrs/week US Pacific time



WHAT YOU'LL GET:

- $4-5/hr USD weekly via EasyPay

- AI-assisted call prep workflow — we'll teach you how to use ChatGPT/Claude to research a patient's chart in 30 seconds before you dial

- Real growth path: AR Specialist ? Senior AR ? Billing Manager

- Stable long-term role at a growing US practice

- Work with a founder who actually answers your messages



HOW TO APPLY:

Apply at 



The application is a 9-question voice screener with real patient scenarios:

- "I can't afford to pay" — walk through your exact response

- A sobbing patient ("my husband just died") — what do you do

- "You guys overcharged me, I'm not paying" — de-escalate

- Insurance dispute — explain a denial in plain English

- ...and 5 more



Plan ~30 min. Strong candidates get a reply within 48hr.



— Dr. Henry Qiu, Founder

5D Smiles, Downey CA

title: Cold Caller / Sales Development Rep — AI Services
description:
HIRING NOW — Outbound Cold Caller | AI Agency | US Market



Simpli AI is a fast-growing AI agency helping HVAC and plumbing contractors in Texas stop losing customers to missed calls. We are expanding and need a sharp, hungry outbound caller to join our team immediately.



Your job is simple. You call business owners in Texas. You spark their interest. You book a 5-minute call with our manager Roy. You do not sell. You do not discuss pricing. You just book the call. Roy handles everything after that.



This is a high volume, high accountability role. We track everything daily. If you are not comfortable being measured on calls made, connects, and demos booked every single day — this role is not for you.



---

WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING



- Making 150 to 200 outbound cold calls per day to US business owners

- Following a proven script that is already working — no improvising

- Handling objections confidently and always attempting a reframe before hanging up

- Booking 5-minute demo calls with qualified business owners

- Logging every call immediately in our CRM

- Sending a daily performance report to your manager every day by 6pm CST



---



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



 Fluent English with a neutral or minimal accent — non negotiable. You will be speaking with US business owners every day and must sound confident and clear.



Proven high volume outbound cold calling experience — 150 to 200 calls per day in a previous role. We will ask for proof.



Experience calling US businesses specifically — not surveys, not inbound, not chat support. Real outbound cold calls to business owners.



 GoHighLevel CRM experience — you know how to log calls, update pipelines, and manage leads without being told twice.



Familiarity with AI tools and technology — you understand what we sell and can speak about it naturally.



Hungry, consistent, and accountable — you do not need hand-holding. You show up, you dial, you report.



---



WHAT WE OFFER



- Competitive base pay paid biweekly

- Performance bonuses for demos booked

- Direct access to your manager for support and feedback

- A proven script, trained objection handling, and full onboarding

- Long term opportunity to grow with the agency as we scale



---



TO APPLY



Send your application with the following or you will not be considered:



1. A short voice recording introducing yourself — 30 to 60 seconds. We need to hear your English before anything else.

2. Your previous cold calling experience including how many calls per day you made and what industry you called.

3. Confirm you have used GoHighLevel CRM.

4. Your availability and timezone.



Applications without a voice recording will not be reviewed. We are hiring immediately and will move fast with the right person.
